data.title: Akamai Stock Down Sharply After Earnings. It's a 'Great Story, But Need to Execute.' — Barrons.com
data.description:
Karishma Vanjani

Akamai Technologies stock took a hit Friday morning as investors likely remain unimpressed with its near-term story.

On Thursday, the cloud services company Akamai posted adjusted earnings of $1.66 a share for the fourth quarter on revenue of $1.02 billion. Earnings were ahead of the FactSet consensus of $1.52 a share, while revenue was in line.

The outlook is weighing on shares more. Akamai is a major player in the delivery of internet content, but the segment has seen large revenue declines due to a broader slowdown in internet traffic. Akamai has been shifting revenue to its security and computing segment.

Akamai projected 2025 revenue between $4 billion and $4.20 billion, below the average Wall Street estimate of $4.26 billion.

It expects a year-over-year decline of 10% in delivery revenue in 2025, better than the 15% fall in 2024 and declines seen in prior years as more customers have been willing to sign multiyear contracts with predictable pricing.

It also established ambitious three- to five-year goals that included an inorganic total revenue growth of over 10%, substantially above the 2.7% growth expected in 2025 at the midpoint.

J.P. Morgan has a Sell rating on the stock. "Our overall conclusion is that while we laud the long-term targets, which will be commendable if achieved, growth in the near-term remains challenged and below where Akamai and investors want it to be," the firm said, lowering its target for price to $72 from $76.

Guggenheim's analyst John DiFucci titled his note 'Great Story, But Need to Execute.' DiFucci has a Buy rating on the stock and encourages investors to remain patient. He expects the stock to go to $133.

The stock was down 9.3% in premarket trading to $88.90.

Write to Karishma Vanjani at karishma.vanjani@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Dubai Insurance Co Posts Lower FY24 Profit; Insurance Revenue Up
data.description:
Dubai Insurance Co. (DFM:DIN) filed Friday its 2024 results that showed a year-over-year profit decline and insurance revenue increase.

Profit after tax for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 132.3 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 141.9 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 1.323 dirhams from 1.374 dirhams.

The Dubai-listed insurer's insurance revenue was 2.99 billion dirhams, compared with 2.15 billion dirhams a year ago.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 9 AM ET
data.description:
Dow Futures Fall While China Tech Stocks Rally

Dow industrials futures fell amid a selloff in UnitedHealth after the WSJ reported the Justice Department was investigating it.

Chinese Stocks Rally on Strong Alibaba Earnings, DeepSeek Optimism

Upbeat earnings by big tech companies have added to a recent rally driven by Xi Jinping's meeting with top corporate executives and the AI hype around DeepSeek.

In the Trump Era, Flying Green Doesn't Matter Anymore

The president's endorsement has pushed up air-taxi stocks, but development of all types of zero-emission aircraft is falling by the wayside.

Why Is Warren Buffett Hoarding So Much Cash?

Investors are poised to study the Berkshire Hathaway chairman's annual letter for insights about the stock market.

The Latest Dilemma Facing Finance Chiefs: What to Tell Investors About Tariffs

CFOs are weighing how much information to share about the impact of tariffs, especially as many are yet to take effect and the landscape continues to evolve.

Private Equity Sees Deal Pipelines Filling In as Year Begins

Private-equity executives entered 2025 with high hopes for a dealmaking recovery that is lifting payouts to fund investors and powering fundraising.

Trump's Energy Plans Are Creating Surprising Opportunities

It's not all about oil, one of the president's top priorities. Why natural gas leads our list of the industry's most promising investments. Even some solar and wind stocks could climb.

Tech stocks seem to have ended their 'difficult relationship' with the bond market

At least for now, U.S. large-cap technology and growth stocks appear "free of their difficult relationship with long-term yields," according to DataTrek Research.

Europe's Economic Stagnation Persists, Latest Business Surveys Say

The eurozone economy continued to flatline in February, torn between signs of revival in Germany and sharp decline in France.

French Industry Sentiment Improves Despite Tariff Threat

The mood in France's factories brightened a little this month as borrowing costs come down, and despite the threat of tariffs on exports to the U.S.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 9 AM ET
data.description:
Coinbase Says SEC Intends to Drop Lawsuit Against Crypto Exchange

The agency had sought to oversee the company as a stock market.

DOJ Investigates Medicare Billing Practices at UnitedHealth

The Justice Department is probing diagnoses and practices that triggered extra payments to UnitedHealth's Medicare Advantage plans, people familiar with the matter say.

Trump Is Planning to Take Control of the Postal Service, Officials Say

The president is seeking to disband the mail provider's governing board and put the USPS under the control of the Commerce Department.

Moody's Cuts Nissan Motor's Rating to Junk Status

Moody's said the rating action reflects the Japanese automaker's weak profitability driven by slowing demand for its aging model portfolio.

Thyssenkrupp Says Potential Buyer of HKM Joint Venture Broke Off Talks

The industrial group said discussions with CE Capital Partners about buying Huettenwerke Krupp Mannesmann had collapsed.

The Toughest Job in Fashion? Design Head for a Luxury Brand.

High-end brands are churning through creative directors as they try to revive sales.

U.K. Fines Banks Over Information Sharing on Government Bonds

The competition regulator fined Citi, HSBC, Morgan Stanley and Royal Bank of Canada $132 million after a probe into the exchange of sensitive information about government bonds.

ESPN, MLB Agree to End TV Deal

The sports giant sought to reduce the rights fees it paid the league; MLB to seek new rights deal for 2026 season.

Newmont Alert to Cost Risks From Tariff-Fueled Currency Swings

Newmont, the world's biggest gold producer, doesn't anticipate an upheaval in sales from proposed U.S. tariffs given it mostly ships gold to buyers elsewhere.

Standard Chartered Net Profit Falls, Announces $1.5 Billion Buyback

The London-based bank, which generates much of its profit in Asia, said underlying profit before tax declined 0.9% to $1.05 billion.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0910 ET - U.S. natural gas picks up from yesterday's retreat with a widening inventory deficit keeping futures well above $4/mmBtu across the curve. This week's Arctic weather is expected to be reflected in a seventh straight triple-digit storage draw in next week's EIA report, and winter conditions are seen extending into March. "After the warm spell, some predict it will get cold again. This expectation is maintaining the demand for natural gas," Phil Flynn of the Price Futures Group says in a note. The Nymex front month is up 2.9% at $4.273/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0855 ET - With potash appearing to make a comeback, Nutrien is positioned to benefit. In a Raymond James report, Steve Hansen says he sees tangible signs of life in the global potash markets, including solid demand and rising prices across most global regions. Nutrien management also sees pricing momentum continuing in 2025, Hansen says, thanks to strong grower affordability as crop prices rise, strong demand from Brazil and Southeast Asia, and balanced inventory levels across key markets such as China, India and Southeast Asia. Hansen says that other key providers in Laos and Russia are currently facing operational challenges and maintenance outages. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0717 ET - Base metal prices fall, with LME three-month copper down 0.2% at $9,535.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 1.3% at $2,695 a ton. However, the base metals complex is set to end the week higher on mounting concerns of trade wars and a generally weaker U.S. dollar, with copper up 0.7% on week and aluminum up 2.3% on week. Among North American copper miners, 2024 copper production was on average 5% below original guidance, and 2025 guidance was 5% below consensus estimates, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. This likely reflects more conservatism on guidance from miners and more challenging mining. Some recent positive signs around U.S.-China relations are constructive for metals and the fundamentals for copper look solid, though uncertainty remains short-term, RBC adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0640 ET - Gold futures slip, but remain close to record highs. Futures are down 0.25% at $2,948.70 a troy ounce after a fresh high of $2,973.40/oz in the prior session. The precious metal is set to finish the week higher on safe-haven demand and attention from U.S. President Trump's administration, SP Angel analysts say in a note. Trump insisted he would "make sure the gold is there" when asked about concerns over the Fort Knox gold supply and the rally gained steam after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent mentioned monetizing the U.S. balance sheet, SP Angel says. However, Bessent dismissed revaluing U.S. gold reserves, and ruled out gold as an asset for a new U.S. sovereign wealth fund, SP Angel writes. Consistent central bank purchases and Chinese consumer buying also continue to support gold, SP Angel adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0516 ET - Palm oil ended higher, supported by an improving regional economic landscape and the potential for short-covering activities ahead of the weekend, which could provide a supportive tone to the market, according to Kenanga Futures analysts in a commentary. Looking ahead, the next key resistance is at MYR4,700/ton, according to Phillip Nova analyst Lim Tai An. A successful breakout above this level could propel prices to MYR4,770/ton, while failure to break through would likely lead to a retracement towards the MYR4,550 level, the analyst says. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery rose MYR22 to MYR4,664/ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0511 ET - OPEC+ is expected to delay the output hike planned for early April amid an uncertain economic and geopolitical outlook, according to analysts at ANZ Research. "The downside for prices is high if OPEC proceeds with production hikes," they say. The cartel and its allies face potential challenges from shifting U.S. policy and risks of a trade war, while renewed maximum pressure on Iran, sanctions on Russia and the halt of Venezuelan crude oil purchases add to the complexity. At the same time, rising fiscal pressure pushes OPEC members to focus on maximizing revenue, according to ANZ. "OPEC's ability to phase out production cuts without disrupting the oil market is getting increasingly difficult," the analysts say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0417 ET - Gold futures slide but remain on-track for weekly gains on safe-haven demand. Futures are down 0.6% at $2,939.70 a troy ounce, but remain up 1.4% on week. The precious metal set a fresh record high of $2,973.40/oz in the prior session, and a test of $3,000/oz is starting to look like a question of "when" rather than "if," Pepperstone's Michael Brown says in a note. The $3,000 mark might give the bulls a point to reassess gold's momentum and perhaps take some profit, but upward momentum should continue with safe-haven demand continuing to linger, Brown writes. President Trump's tariff threats, lingering trade and economic uncertainty and persistent geopolitical tensions are supporting the flight to safe-haven assets like gold. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0407 ET - Crude futures edge lower in early trade but are still poised for weekly gains of more than 1.5% on rising supply concerns and a weaker U.S. dollar. Prices are supported by disruptions to Kazakhstan's oil flows after a Ukrainian drone attack on a major pipeline in Russia and prospects that OPEC+ could delay its output hike plans. The market is also keeping a close eye on developments in U.S.-Russia talks to end the war in Ukraine. Meanwhile, the latest data showed U.S. crude stockpiles rose more than expected last week, while gasoline inventories and distillate fuel stocks fell. According to analysts at Saxo, "the market continues to lack a clear direction, with supply disruptions in Kazakhstan and the OPEC+ production increase delay being offset by global demand worries." Brent crude is down 0.7% to $75.93 a barrel, while WTI falls 0.8% to $71.92 a barrel. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0204 ET - Comex gold futures are likely extending consolidation, based on the daily chart, RHB Retail Research's Joseph Chai says in a research report. The relative strength index is now moving sideways, indicating that momentum remains neutral, the analyst notes. Hence, the commodity is likely trading sideways for consolidation. Gold is also maintaining a bullish technical setup, trading above 20- and 50-day simple moving averages, the analyst says. As long as the precious metal stays above support at the $2,800/oz level, RHB will retain a positive trading bias for the commodity. Spot gold is 0.3% lower at $2,929.74/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2147 ET - Palm oil prices rise in Asian trading, tracking gains in soybean oil overnight on the Chicago Board of Trade. Palm oil's recent rally has pushed it to a premium over soybean oil, which could weigh on demand, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. That trend may persist amid expectations of a strong U.S. soy harvest, it adds. Technically, with crude palm oil futures continuing to signal bullish momentum, prices may test the MYR4,750-a-ton resistance level, with pullbacks offering buying opportunities, it says. AmInvestment Bank sees support for CPO prices at 4,613 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,747 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery rises 62 ringgit to 4,704 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2145 ET - Iron-ore prices are higher in early Asian trade amid tight supply. The Australian cyclone likely affected 7 million tons of iron-ore shipment, weighing on its supply, Nanhua Futures says in a research note. On the demand front, market sentiment is improving on the prospect of better macroeconomic performance in China, it adds. Investors will pay attention to China's National People's Congress in early March for any further property supportive measures. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 1.6% higher at 839.50 yuan a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2133 ET - Aluminum edges higher in early Asian trade on reports that the European Union has backed a ban on Russian aluminum, say ANZ research analysts in a commentary. EU Ambassadors have agreed to move forward with a fresh package of sanctions against the key supplier, the analysts say noting media reports. These include a gradual ban on imports, restrictions on dozens of Russian vessels and prohibiting about a dozen banks from accessing the SWIFT international payment system, the analysts add. The three-month LME Aluminum contract is 0.1% higher at $2,729.50 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Else Nutrition Closes US$300,000 Private Placement
data.description:
Else Nutrition on Friday closed a third tranche of a private placement following an amended and restated convertible security funding agreement with Lind Global Fund II, an entity managed by The Lind Partners, for proceeds of US$300,000.

The company issued a convertible security, after the closing, with a two-year term and a face value of US$375,000, and 62.2 million common share purchase warrants exercisable into common shares for a four-year period from the date of issuance at an exercise price of $0.0201 per share.

Else Nutrition added that the face value of the third convertible security can be increased by US$375,000 for US$300,000 on or before April 1, following the funding agreement.

The company can also draw an additional $300,000 on or before July 1, 2024 in exchange for the issuance of an additional convertible security with a face value of US$375,000. This convertible security, if issued, may be increased on or before Oct. 1, by US$375,000 for US$300,000, but warrants would not be issued.

Proceeds from the sale of the third and fourth convertible securities, if issued, will be applied to general working capital.

data.title: Flowers Foods Completes Simple Mills Acquisition
data.description:
Flowers Foods said Friday it has completed its acquisition of Simple Mills, a producer of crackers, cookies, snack bars, and baking mixes.

The company previously said it agreed to acquire Simple Mills for $795 million in cash from Katlin Smith, Simple Mills management, Vestar Capital Partners, and initial angel investors.

Flowers Foods also said in January it expects the acquisition to be immediately accretive to net sales and to earnings per share in 2026.

FLO shares were up 0.4% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Canada Retail Sales Slide After Jumping 2.5% in December — Update
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Canadians look to have pared back their spending at the start of the year, tightening their belts after the strongest outlay at retailers for more than two years in December.

An advance estimate of retail receipts indicates sales fell 0.4% in January, Statistics Canada said Friday.

The pullback could hint at some hesitancy given clouds over Canada's economy, or it could suggest consumers took a breather, snapping a six-month run of rising sales.

Sales in December jumped a stronger-than-expected 2.5% from the month before to a seasonally adjusted 69.59 billion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of about $49.09 billion. Compared with a year earlier, retailers saw a 3.9% jump in sales in the last month of 2024.

December's strength was broad, materializing across retail sectors and in each Canadian province, and supported signs that the economy picked up in the final quarter.

Statistics Canada offered no details with the estimate for January, which was based on the responses of roughly 58% of retailers surveyed and which will be revised.

A lull in sales was expected after spending surged, buoyed in part by the start in mid-December of a federal government tax break on items including children's clothing, toys and restaurant meals that continued through mid-February.

Royal Bank of Canada's analysis of anonymized card transactions by Canadian customers showed significantly less was spent in January on clothing and electronics even as grocery baskets got a little lighter. One bright spot in the lender's January data was home-related spending on furniture and household electronics, as well as on building materials.

A string of six interest-rate cuts by the Bank of Canada since June has lowered borrowing costs and helped drive household spending, offsetting weakness in business investment. The federal tax holiday helped keep inflation in check in January, with inflation staying below the central bank's 2% target even amid upward pressure from rising energy costs.

Still, the outlook for Canada's economy has become more uncertain under the looming threat from the White House of hefty tariffs on Canadian exports and President Trump's repeated calls for the country to become the 51st U.S. state. Business surveys indicate companies are more hesitant, with some delaying investment decisions and looking at supply chains in case tariffs hit.

Retailers have benefited from consumer resilience, with sales increasing 2.4% in the final quarter of last year, an acceleration in the pace from the quarter before. Sales over the course of the year were up 1.3% after weakness in the first half of 2024.

December sales came in stronger than the 1.6% advance economists expected and what the data agency had predicted. There were gains in each of the nine retail segments tracked by Statistics Canada, and overall sales volumes also were up solidly with a 2.5% rise for the month.

Motor-vehicle and parts dealers were a big driver for the month, with new-car sales up for a third consecutive month. Sales at gas stations and fuel vendors also showed a big increase thanks to higher prices.

Core sales, which strip out car and auto-parts dealers and gas stations, were up a similar 2.5% after weakness in November.

Food-and-beverage retailers notched a 3.5% increase in sales from the prior month, with gains notched at supermarkets and higher receipts at beer, wine and liquor stores. Higher sales were also recorded at general merchandise retailers and clothing, accessories and leather-goods retailers in December.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Sector Update: Energy
data.description:
Energy stocks declined premarket Friday as the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) fell by 0.2% recently.

The United States Oil Fund (USO) was down 0.8% and the United States Natural Gas Fund (UNG) was over 2% higher.

Front-month US West Texas Intermediate crude oil was down 0.5% at $72.09 per barrel at the New York Mercantile Exchange. Global benchmark North Sea Brent crude oil lost 0.5% to $76.12 per barrel, and natural gas futures were 2.1% higher at $4.24 per 1 million British Thermal Units.

Solaris Energy Infrastructure shares were up over 15% after the company reported an increase in Q4 revenue, while adjusted earnings declined.

data.title: Knife River Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Knife River Corporation, a leading player in the construction materials industry, has released its 2024 10-K report, showcasing significant financial growth, strategic acquisitions, and operational advancements. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a competitive market.

Financial Highlights

Knife River Corp reported robust financial performance for 2024, driven by strategic pricing initiatives and increased public-agency work. Key financial metrics include:

  • Revenue: $2,899.0 million, a 2% increase from 2023.
  • Gross Profit: $569.8 million, a 6% improvement with a gross margin increase of 70 basis points.
  • Operating Income: $316.2 million, a 7% rise due to improved bid margins and favorable project execution.
  • Net Income: $201.7 million, a 10% increase, reflecting a net income margin of 7.0%.

Business Highlights

Knife River Corp's operational performance in 2024 was marked by strong sales across its diverse product lines and geographical segments:

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through several product lines, with contracting services being the largest segment, accounting for 38.6% of total revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: The Central segment generated the highest revenue of $818.1 million, showcasing the company's strong presence across multiple U.S. regions.
  • Sales Units: In 2024, Knife River sold 31.8 million tons of aggregates, 3.5 million cubic yards of ready-mix concrete, and 6.5 million tons of asphalt.
  • New Production Launches: The acquisition of Albina Asphalt expanded the company's liquid asphalt production capacity, enhancing its operational footprint in Washington, Oregon, and California.
  • Future Outlook: Knife River plans to continue its growth strategy through organic expansion and strategic acquisitions, with the acquisition of Strata Corporation expected to strengthen its market position in North Dakota and northwestern Minnesota in 2025.

Strategic Initiatives

Knife River Corp has been actively pursuing growth through strategic initiatives and capital management activities:

  • Growth Through Acquisitions: The company invested $131.0 million in 2024 to close on six acquisitions, including Albina Asphalt, to expand its liquid asphalt operations.
  • EDGE Initiatives: Implementing EDGE initiatives to enhance margins and operational efficiencies, focusing on training and process improvements across its facilities.
  • Capital Management: Issued $425.0 million of 7.75% senior notes due in 2031 and entered into a senior secured credit agreement, including a $275.0 million term loan and a $350.0 million revolving credit facility. Plans to increase the revolving credit facility to $500 million and extend its maturity to 2030.
  • Future Outlook: Knife River aims to fund the acquisition of Strata Corporation and other growth initiatives through a new $500 million Term Loan B facility, supporting ongoing capital needs and strategic investments.

Challenges and Risks

Knife River Corp faces several challenges and risks inherent to the construction materials industry:

  • Operational Risks: Seasonal and weather-related disruptions can impact production and sales. The company also faces risks related to customer nonpayment and nonperformance.
  • Regulatory Risks: Extensive environmental regulations could increase operational costs and limit business plans. New laws, such as California's climate disclosure requirements, may impose additional compliance burdens.
  • Market Risks: Economic volatility and changes in government infrastructure spending can significantly impact demand. The company is also exposed to fluctuations in commodity prices.
  • Emerging Risks: Integration of artificial intelligence and technology disruptions pose new challenges, including potential cybersecurity threats and the need for continuous system upgrades.
  • Financial Risks: Substantial indebtedness could limit financial flexibility and increase vulnerability to economic downturns. The planned acquisition of Strata Corporation involves significant debt financing, impacting liquidity and financial risk.

Management is focused on executing the EDGE strategy, emphasizing EBITDA margin improvement, disciplined capital allocation, growth through acquisitions, and operational excellence to mitigate these risks.

SEC Filing: Knife River Corp [ KNF ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Intra-Cellular Therapies, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Intra-Cellular Therapies, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company focused on the development and commercialization of innovative therapies for central nervous system (CNS) disorders, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive biopharmaceutical landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues, net: $680.9 million, reflecting a 47% increase from the previous year due to continued growth in sales volume of CAPLYTA for the treatment of schizophrenia and bipolar depression.
  • Loss from operations: $(116.7) million, an improvement from the previous year's loss of $(159.4) million, primarily due to increased net product sales.
  • Net loss: $(74.7) million, compared to $(139.7) million in the prior year, indicating a reduction in net loss driven by higher revenues.
  • Net cash used in operating activities: $(73.2) million, a decrease from $(124.2) million in the previous year, primarily due to increased net product sales.
  • Net cash provided by financing activities: $567.5 million, significantly higher than the previous year's $17.8 million, mainly due to the completion of an underwritten public offering of shares.

Business Highlights

  • Acquisition Agreement: The company entered into a definitive agreement with Johnson & Johnson for acquisition at $132 per share, totaling approximately $14.6 billion, expected to close in 2025 subject to customary conditions.
  • Product Launch - CAPLYTA: CAPLYTA was launched for the treatment of schizophrenia in March 2020 and for bipolar depression in December 2021. Additional dosage strengths were launched in August 2022 for special populations.
  • Pipeline Development: The company is advancing several product candidates, including lumateperone for major depressive disorder (MDD) and ITI-1284 for generalized anxiety disorder and Alzheimer's-related conditions.
  • Clinical Trials: Positive results were announced from Phase 3 trials for lumateperone as adjunctive therapy for MDD, with a supplemental new drug application submitted to the FDA in December 2024.
  • Manufacturing Agreements: The company renewed its supply agreement with Siegfried for API production until January 2026 and extended its agreement with Lonza until December 2028, ensuring continued supply for lumateperone.
  • Sales Force Expansion: The company expanded its sales force in 2024 and continues to do so in 2025 to support the commercialization of CAPLYTA and potential new indications.
  • Regulatory Developments: The FDA accepted the supplemental new drug application for lumateperone for MDD in early 2025, with ongoing efforts to obtain approval for additional indications.
  • Intellectual Property: The company holds a robust patent portfolio with 142 patent families, including 158 issued U.S. patents, providing protection for its compounds and formulations.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: The company is committed to environmental responsibility, focusing on waste management, energy efficiency, and sustainable manufacturing processes.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to expand the commercial potential of lumateperone, advance its pipeline candidates, and explore strategic alliances for international markets.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company entered into a definitive agreement with Johnson & Johnson for an acquisition valued at approximately $14.6 billion, which is expected to close in 2025. This strategic move aims to enhance the company's market position and leverage Johnson & Johnson's resources for future growth. Additionally, the company is expanding its clinical trial programs and pre-clinical development activities, particularly focusing on the development of lumateperone for new indications and other product candidates like ITI-1284 and PDE1 inhibitors.
  • Capital Management: The company completed an underwritten public offering of common stock in April 2024, raising approximately $543.1 million in net proceeds. This capital is intended to support ongoing operations, including the commercialization of CAPLYTA and the expansion of clinical programs. The company maintains a strong liquidity position with cash, cash equivalents, and investment securities totaling approximately $1.0 billion as of December 31, 2024. The company has not declared or paid any cash dividends and intends to retain future earnings to finance business growth.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates the merger with Johnson & Johnson to be completed in 2025, which will provide additional resources and strategic opportunities. The company plans to continue expanding its sales force and infrastructure to support the potential approval of lumateperone for major depressive disorder. Additionally, the company is exploring further financing opportunities to support its strategic initiatives, including potential public or private sales of equity or debt securities, subject to market conditions and the proposed merger.

Challenges and Risks

  • Merger Risks: The company faces significant risks related to the pending merger with Johnson & Johnson, including the possibility that the merger may not be completed within the anticipated timeframe or at all. This could adversely affect the company's business, financial results, and market price of its common stock. The merger's pendency could also disrupt business operations, affect employee retention, and lead to negative publicity and stockholder litigation.
  • Commercialization Risks: The company is heavily reliant on the successful commercialization of CAPLYTA, its only approved drug, for the treatment of schizophrenia and bipolar depression in adults. Failure to effectively expand its commercialization could hinder the company's ability to achieve profitability. Additionally, the company faces challenges in obtaining regulatory approval for lumateperone for other indications, which would limit commercial revenues.
  • Operational Risks: Operational risks include reliance on third-party manufacturers for the supply of lumateperone and other product candidates. Any failure by these manufacturers to comply with regulatory requirements or to meet the company's needs could result in significant delays and increased costs.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are heightened by ongoing obligations and restrictions related to CAPLYTA, which may result in significant expenses and limit the company's ability to commercialize its products. The company also faces potential safety issues with its product candidates or approved products, which could lead to regulatory delays or product withdrawal.
  • Market Risks: Market risks include the potential for increased competition, which could undermine the company's market position. Many competitors have greater resources, which could negatively impact the company's commercial opportunities.
  • Funding Risks: Management acknowledges the need for additional funding to support ongoing operations and commercialization efforts. The company may require further capital to continue clinical development and commercialization activities, and there is no guarantee that such funding will be available on acceptable terms.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The company is also exposed to risks related to cybersecurity incidents, which could compromise sensitive information and disrupt business operations. The increasing frequency and sophistication of cyber-attacks pose a significant threat to the company's data integrity and operational continuity.
  • Market Risk Disclosures: The company is subject to market risks related to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could adversely affect financial results. Additionally, changes in tax laws, such as the Inflation Reduction Act, could impact the company's financial condition and operations.

data.title: ICHOR HOLDINGS, LTD. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
ICHOR HOLDINGS, LTD., a key player in the semiconductor capital equipment industry, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a highly cyclical market. ICHOR HOLDINGS specializes in the design, engineering, and manufacturing of critical fluid delivery subsystems and components essential for semiconductor manufacturing processes.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $849.04 million, reflecting a 4.7% increase from 2023 due to increased customer demand within the semiconductor capital equipment industry.
  • Gross profit: $103.33 million, with a gross margin of 12.2%, slightly down from 12.7% in 2023 due to unfavorable sales mix and increased factory labor and overhead costs.
  • Operating loss: $(7.64) million, an improvement from $(10.90) million in 2023, driven by reduced amortization of intangible assets and share-based compensation expenses.
  • Net loss: $(20.82) million, improved from $(42.99) million in 2023, primarily due to a decrease in interest expense and income tax expense.
  • Diluted EPS: $(0.64), compared to $(1.47) in 2023, reflecting the overall improvement in net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company primarily generates revenue from the design, engineering, and manufacturing of critical fluid delivery subsystems and components for semiconductor capital equipment. These include gas and chemical delivery systems, which are essential for semiconductor manufacturing processes.
  • Customer Concentration: The company’s sales are highly concentrated, with two major customers, Lam Research and Applied Materials, accounting for a combined 73% of total sales in 2024. This highlights the company's reliance on a small number of large customers in the semiconductor equipment market.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates globally with production facilities in the United States, Singapore, Malaysia, the United Kingdom, Korea, and Mexico. This global footprint allows the company to strategically position itself near major customers and respond quickly to their needs.
  • New Product Launches: The company continues to innovate in fluid delivery systems, focusing on developing new products that meet the evolving needs of semiconductor OEMs. This includes advancements in gas and chemical delivery systems to support next-generation semiconductor manufacturing processes.
  • Operational Excellence: The company emphasizes its operational excellence, with a focus on reducing manufacturing cycle times and maintaining flexibility to accommodate customer needs. This includes strategic investments in manufacturing systems and processes to enhance efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth opportunities by expanding its market share within existing customers and exploring new markets. It aims to leverage its engineering expertise and customer relationships to drive innovation and maintain its competitive edge in the fluid delivery subsystem market.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company has focused on strategic initiatives to enhance its financial position and operational efficiency. This includes a global reduction-in-force program to align resources with current business levels, and the consolidation of one of its U.S.-based manufacturing facilities to optimize operations. Additionally, the company has been actively managing its acquisition pipeline, incurring transaction-related costs to explore potential growth opportunities.
  • Capital Management: In 2024, the company issued 3.8 million ordinary shares in an underwritten public offering, raising net proceeds of $136.7 million. This capital was used to pay down $120.6 million of its credit facilities, reflecting a strategic move to reduce leverage. The company also maintained a valuation allowance against its U.S. federal and state deferred tax assets, impacting its tax expense and net income calculations. No dividends were declared, and there were no equity repurchases or stock splits during the period.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates that demand for semiconductors and related capital equipment will return to growth, driven by the long-term need for increased semiconductor production capacity and advanced process technologies. It plans to continue monitoring global trade environments and macroeconomic conditions to adjust its strategic initiatives accordingly. The company remains committed to maintaining sufficient liquidity to support its operations and strategic goals over the next 12 months and beyond.

Challenges and Risks

  • Economic and Strategic Risks: The semiconductor industry is subject to cyclical downturns, which can lead to decreased demand for the company's products and services. The company must align its cost structure with market conditions and manage its supply chain effectively to mitigate these risks. Additionally, geopolitical instability and macroeconomic factors such as high inflation and interest rates pose challenges to the company's operations.
  • Business and Operational Risks: The manufacturing process is highly complex, and any defects in products could damage the company's reputation and lead to costly litigation. The company also faces supply chain disruptions and relies on a limited number of suppliers, which could impact production output and increase costs. Furthermore, the company is exposed to risks related to information technology system failures and cybersecurity threats, which could result in significant financial losses.
  • Legal and Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to various U.S. and international laws regarding privacy, data protection, and environmental regulations. Non-compliance with these regulations could result in significant fines and operational disruptions. Changes in trade policies and tariffs, particularly those affecting China, could also adversely impact the company's business.
  • Liquidity and Capital Resources Risks: The company has a substantial amount of indebtedness, which could limit its business flexibility and ability to respond to market changes. The interest expense associated with variable rate debt could increase if interest rates rise, affecting the company's financial condition.
  • Ordinary Share Ownership Risks: The company's quarterly sales and operating results are subject to significant fluctuations, which may cause volatility in its share price. Anti-takeover provisions in the company's articles of association could also adversely affect shareholder rights.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to align cost structures with market conditions and effectively manage the supply chain as critical to mitigating the impact of cyclical downturns in the semiconductor industry. The company is also focused on maintaining strong relationships with key OEM customers to ensure continued business.
  • Market Risk: The company is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, particularly with the Singapore dollar, Malaysian ringgit, Korean won, British pound, Mexican peso, and euro. These fluctuations could cause operating results to vary significantly from period to period, impacting the company's financial performance.

SEC Filing: ICHOR HOLDINGS, LTD. [ ICHR ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Canadian Retail Sales Unlikely to Shift BoC Thinking — Market Talk
data.description: With retail sales in Canada faltering in January after the previous month's jump, the needle doesn't move for the Bank of Canada's rate decision next month, Capital Economics' Stephen Brown says. He expects the BoC's call will hinge on the 4Q GDP release next week and whether U.S. tariffs scheduled for March 4 come into effect or not. Still, the economist notes the upside surprise in retail sales volumes in December, which suggests monthly GDP rose 0.3% on-month rather than the 0.2% he previously anticipated. That offers a better hand-off to the new year, though likely only pushed 4Q GDP growth to 1.9% annualized from the 1.8% previously expected, he says. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

data.title: COHEN & STEERS, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Cohen & Steers, Inc., a global investment manager specializing in real assets and alternative income, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive investment management industry. Founded in 1986 and headquartered in New York City, Cohen & Steers operates globally with offices in London, Dublin, Hong Kong, Tokyo, and Singapore.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $517.4 million, increased by 5.7% from the previous year primarily due to higher average assets under management.

Operating Income: $172.9 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year with an operating margin of 33.4%.

Net Income Attributable to Common Stockholders: $151.3 million, up from $129.0 million in the prior year, indicating improved profitability.

Diluted Earnings Per Share: $2.97, compared to $2.60 in the previous year, showing an increase in earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Cohen & Steers, founded in 1986, is a global investment manager specializing in real assets and alternative income, including listed and private real estate, preferred securities, infrastructure, resource equities, commodities, and multi-strategy solutions. The company is headquartered in New York City with offices in London, Dublin, Hong Kong, Tokyo, and Singapore.

Investment Vehicles: The company manages three types of investment vehicles: open-end funds, institutional accounts, and closed-end funds. Open-end funds offer and issue new shares continuously, institutional accounts are managed for institutional clients, and closed-end funds have a fixed number of shares listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

New Product Launches: In 2024, Cohen & Steers launched its first active exchange-traded funds (ETFs) with three strategies: U.S. real estate securities, preferred securities, and natural resource equities.

Investment Strategies: The company's core investment strategies include U.S. Real Estate Securities, Global/International Real Estate Securities, Private Real Estate, Preferred Securities, Global Listed Infrastructure, Global Natural Resource Equities, and Real Assets Multi-Strategy.

Geographical Performance: The company operates globally, with investment teams located in New York, London, and Hong Kong, and serves institutional and individual investors worldwide.

Regulation: Cohen & Steers is subject to regulation under U.S. federal and state laws, as well as applicable laws in other jurisdictions where it operates. Key subsidiaries are registered with various regulatory bodies, including the SEC, FCA, SFC, and others.

Human Capital: As of December 31, 2024, Cohen & Steers had 411 full-time employees globally, with 36% being women and 33% of U.S. employees being people of color. The company emphasizes talent management, diversity, and inclusion.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in real assets and alternative income investments, leveraging its global presence and expertise in managing diverse investment strategies.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, the company launched its first ETFs, making seed investments of approximately $49.8 million to support this initiative. Additionally, the company issued 1,007,057 shares of common stock through an offering, raising approximately $68.5 million in net proceeds, which are intended for general corporate purposes, including seeding track record strategies and investment vehicles.

Capital Management: The company maintained a strong liquidity position with net liquid assets of $331.5 million as of December 31, 2024. It has a $100 million senior unsecured revolving credit facility maturing on January 20, 2026, providing financial flexibility. The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.62 per share, payable on March 13, 2025. During the three months ended December 31, 2024, the company repurchased 15,283 shares to satisfy employee tax withholding obligations related to restricted stock units.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue supporting growth initiatives through capital investments, including seeding new strategies and investment vehicles. It has committed to invest up to $175 million in certain investment vehicles, with $80 million remaining unfunded as of December 31, 2024. The company anticipates paying dividends subject to board approval, considering economic conditions and strategic plans.

Challenges and Risks

Market Risks: A decline in the performance or value of real estate and preferred securities could adversely affect assets under management and revenue. The company is also exposed to risks from global economic conditions, interest rate changes, and geopolitical events.

Operational Risks: The loss of key personnel, cybersecurity threats, and reliance on third-party vendors pose significant operational risks. The company’s ability to manage succession planning and maintain effective information security is critical.

Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to extensive regulation in the U.S. and internationally. Changes in laws or regulations, particularly those related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and ESG, could increase compliance costs and impact operations.

Emerging Risks: The use of artificial intelligence and technological advancements presents new risks, including regulatory scrutiny and potential liabilities. The company must adapt to these changes to maintain its competitive position.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis: Management highlights the complex global economic environment, with varying growth levels and geopolitical uncertainties impacting investment opportunities. The company leverages its portfolio management expertise and risk management framework to navigate these challenges.

Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and equity market volatility, which could impact the value of assets under management and revenue. The company uses derivative instruments to mitigate some of these risks, but there is no assurance of their effectiveness.

SEC Filing: COHEN & STEERS, INC. [ CNS ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Desjardins Says Canada GDP Tracking Higher Than BoC's Latest Estimate After December Retail Sales Update
data.description:
Although Canadian consumers seemed to be celebrating the sales tax holiday in December, there wasn't much of a hangover in January, according to Tiago Figueiredo at Desjardins.

Desjardins noted retail receipts rose 2.5% in December, "besting expectations" for a 1.6% advance. After two months of significant gains, automotive sales rose even further, possibly in part due to an expiring incentive to purchase electric vehicles. But, it said, even excluding that category, retail sales increased a "heady" 2.7%, well above expectations. Food and beverage retailers led the way higher after a rough November. But, overall, all nine subcategories rose over the month in both nominal and real terms. Desjardins added: "With this breadth, it's likely that consumers who had previously postponed spending in order to take advantage of the GST/HST holiday that began in mid-December made up for it in the second half of the month." Overall, it noted, sales rebounded 2.5% in volumes terms in December, bringing the 3-month annualized rate to 10.2%. As a result, per capita spending is increasing both in nominal and real terms after weakness earlier in 2024.

According to Statistics Canada's flash estimate, retail sales pulled back just 0.4% in January. Desjardins said with good prices up 0.5% over the month, that means that volumes likely fell roughly 0.9%. However, it added, part of that weakness may simply be due to cold weather keeping shoppers at home.

As a result of the strength in December, Desjardins is now tracking 1.9% GDP growth for Q4, one tick higher than the Bank of Canada's latest estimate. Desjardins continues to forecast 2.0% growth for the first quarter of 2025. It said: "While this reinforces our view that the Bank of Canada will hit the pause button in March, the strength seen may be ephemeral. The tax holiday might be pulling forward some spending that would have occurred later and the most acute concerns about a trade war with the US were likely not captured in this data."

data.title: Loonie Pauses Amid Mixed Data and Trade Uncertainty
data.description:
The Canadian dollar hovered around 1.42 per USD, halting its rebound from a 22-year low of 1.455 on January 31, as investors sifted through a mix of economic signals.

A 0.4% decline in January retail sales, the first drop in seven months, suggested a slowdown in consumer spending following December's robust surge, raising concerns about domestic momentum.

Additionally, ongoing uncertainty over U.S. tariff threats targeting a significant portion of Canadian exports, has dampened the loonie's demand outlook.

Conversely, strong inflationary pressures highlighted the BoC's challenge in balancing growth and controlling prices, with industrial producer prices surging 1.6% and the Raw Materials Price Index jumping 3.7% month-over-month, reducing expectations for further Bank of Canada easing.

Meanwhile, broad U.S. dollar weakness, fueled by President Trump’s less aggressive tariff stance, has alleviated inflation fears and bolstered expectations for potential Fed rate cuts.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Resume Gains — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas picks up from yesterday's retreat with a widening inventory deficit keeping futures well above $4/mmBtu across the curve. This week's Arctic weather is expected to be reflected in a seventh straight triple-digit storage draw in next week's EIA report, and winter conditions are seen extending into March. "After the warm spell, some predict it will get cold again. This expectation is maintaining the demand for natural gas," Phil Flynn of the Price Futures Group says in a note. The Nymex front month is up 2.9% at $4.273/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Canada Retail Sales Slide After Jumping 2.5% in December
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Retail sales in Canada had their biggest climb in roughly two and a half years in December, with the food-and-drink and automotive sectors leading broad gains.

Sales rose 2.5% in December from the month before to a seasonally adjusted 69.59 billion Canadian dollars, the equivalent of about $49.09 billion, Statistics Canada said Friday.

The rise was the strongest since May 2022, and well above the data agency's preliminary estimate for a 1.6% advance in retail trade to end the year. December also marked the sixth straight month of rising sales, following upwardly revised growth of 0.2% in November.

Compared with a year earlier, retail sales in December were 3.9% higher.

The agency said early indications suggest sales fell 0.4% in January, though the figure was based on responses from 57.7% of the retailers surveyed and will be revised.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Leidos Says $267 Million of 2025 Notes Tendered
data.description:
Leidos said Friday that its Leidos Inc. subsidiary's cash tender offer for its 3.625% senior notes due 2025 has expired, with about $267.4 million validly tendered and not withdrawn.

The notes tendered account for around 53.5% of the $500 million total principal amount of the notes, Leidos said.

Holders of the notes who tendered by the expiration Thursday will receive $998.30 for each $1,000 principal amount accepted, plus accrued interest from the last payment date, the company said

Payment for the accepted notes will be made on Feb. 25, the scheduled settlement date, Leidos added.

data.title: Canaccord Genuity Adjusts Insulet Price Target to $324 From $304
data.description: Insulet has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $312.81, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Goldman Sachs' VIP Stock List Outperforms S&P 500 in 2025
data.description:
Goldman Sachs' closely watched list of hedge fund-favorite stocks is off to a strong start this year, outpacing the broader market. According to analysts at the investment bank, their "Hedge Fund Very Important Positions" (VIP) basket has returned 10% year-to-date, more than doubling the S&P 500's 4% gain.

The VIP list tracks the 50 stocks most commonly found in the top 10 holdings of fundamentally-driven hedge funds. Unsurprisingly, six of the "Magnificent 7" tech giants dominate the list: Amazon , Meta Platforms , Microsoft , Nvidia , Alphabet (GOOGL), and Apple (AAPL). Among them, Amazon appeared most frequently, with 120 hedge funds holding it as a top-10 position as of December 31, followed by Meta (80 funds) and Microsoft (75 funds).

However, despite their strong presence, hedge funds trimmed positions in most of the Magnificent 7 stocks, Goldman's analysts noted. The report, which monitored 695 hedge funds, also highlighted new additions to the VIP basket in the fourth quarter, including Delta Air Lines , Pfizer , Reddit , and Workday . Financial stocks like Arthur J Gallagher and Capital One Financial also made the list, even as some funds rotated away from the sector following a post-election rally.

Historically, the VIP basket has outperformed the S&P 500 in 60% of quarters since 2001, with an average quarterly excess return of 52 basis points. The list's solid performance comes as the S&P 500 hovers near all-time highs, fueled by investor optimism over potential looser regulations and lower taxes under the new Trump administration.

data.title: HSBC Adjusts Cognizant Technology Solutions' Price Target to $103 From $92
data.description: Cognizant Technology Solutions has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $90.28, according to analysts polled by FactSet.
